title: Context Engineering and Repository Grounding · Changed-Case Lab
summary: Ground an agent in a large unfamiliar repository without flooding its context. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a repository context map and retrieval plan with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Rank repository files, official docs, generated artifacts, tests, and user statements by authority for the task.
Provide paths, search terms, dependency boundaries, and examples that route efficient discovery.
Separate one-turn constraints from durable repository instructions such as AGENTS.md or CLAUDE.md.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
